RDS203_HW1

Tzu-Hsi (Cece) Jen

2026-04-08

# install.packages("ggrepel")
library(ggrepel)
## Loading required package: ggplot2
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(tidyr)
library(forcats)

1

# Data
data1_input <- read.csv("output_data/Q1_input.csv", header = TRUE)
data1_output <- read.csv("output_data/Q1_output.csv", header = TRUE)

head(data1_input)
##   AgeGroup CohortSize CRC_base CVA_base
## 1    35-39    4567000    91.34  3333.91
## 2    40-44    4214000   168.56  3539.76
## 3    45-49    3690000   295.20  3210.30
## 4    50-54    3092000   401.96  3308.44
## 5    55-59    2534000   456.12  3167.50
## 6    60-64    1979000   494.75  2948.71
head(data1_output)
##   AgeGroup CRC_averted CVA_averted
## 1    35-39    2.537250    396.3356
## 2    40-44    4.754794    424.5697
## 3    45-49    8.418196    287.4699
## 4    50-54   11.482417    296.3794
## 5    55-59   13.090744    284.8745
## 6    60-64   14.030449    214.2617
# Merge datasets
data1 <- data1_input|>
  left_join(data1_output, by = "AgeGroup")

# Proportion of baseline and averted cases
data1_propCases <- data1|>
  mutate(CRC_base_pct = (CRC_base / CohortSize),
         CRC_averted_pct = (CRC_averted / CohortSize),
         
         CVA_base_pct = (CVA_base/ CohortSize),
         CVA_averted_pct = (CVA_averted / CohortSize) 
         )|>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(CRC_base_pct, CRC_averted_pct, CVA_base_pct, CVA_averted_pct),
               names_to = "Type",
               values_to = "Incidence")|>
  mutate(Disease = ifelse(grepl("CRC", Type), "CRC", "CVA"),
         Measure = ifelse(grepl("base", Type), "Baseline", "Averted"))|>
  select(AgeGroup, Disease, Measure, Incidence)
head(data1_propCases)
## # A tibble: 6 × 4
##   AgeGroup Disease Measure    Incidence
##   <chr>    <chr>   <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 35-39    CRC     Baseline 0.00002    
## 2 35-39    CRC     Averted  0.000000556
## 3 35-39    CVA     Baseline 0.00073    
## 4 35-39    CVA     Averted  0.0000868  
## 5 40-44    CRC     Baseline 0.00004    
## 6 40-44    CRC     Averted  0.00000113
# Proportion of averted cases
data1_propAverted <- data1|>
  mutate(CRC_pct_averted = CRC_averted / CRC_base,
         CVA_pct_averted = CVA_averted / CVA_base)|>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(CRC_pct_averted, CVA_pct_averted),
               names_to = "Type",
               values_to = "Averted_prop")|>
  mutate(Disease = ifelse(grepl("CRC", Type), "CRC", "CVA"))|>
  select(AgeGroup, Disease, Averted_prop)
head(data1_propAverted)
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   AgeGroup Disease Averted_prop
##   <chr>    <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 35-39    CRC           0.0278
## 2 35-39    CVA           0.119 
## 3 40-44    CRC           0.0282
## 4 40-44    CVA           0.120 
## 5 45-49    CRC           0.0285
## 6 45-49    CVA           0.0895
# Plot: Incidence
ggplot(data1_propCases, aes(x = AgeGroup, y = Incidence, fill = Measure))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge")+
  facet_wrap(~Disease, scales = "free_y")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(scale = 100))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "grey70",
                               "Averted" = "#FF6347"))+
  labs(x = "Age Group",
       y = "Incidence (%)")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Plot: Averted proportion
ggplot(data1_propAverted, aes(x = AgeGroup, y = Averted_prop, fill = Disease))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(scale = 100))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("CRC" = "#CDAA7D",
                               "CVA" = "#7AC5CD"))+
  labs(x = "Age Group",
       y = "Averted Proportion (%)",
       fill = "Disease")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

2c.

# Load data
data2c <- read.csv("output_data/Q2c.csv", header = TRUE)
head(data2c)
##   t        S        I         R
## 1 0 9999.000 1.000000 0.0000000
## 2 1 9998.700 1.199970 0.1000000
## 3 2 9998.340 1.439917 0.2199970
## 4 3 9997.908 1.727829 0.3639887
## 5 4 9997.390 2.073286 0.5367716
## 6 5 9996.768 2.487781 0.7441002
# Convert to long format
data2c_long <- data2c|>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(S, I, R),
               names_to = "Compartments",
               values_to = "Value")
head(data2c_long)
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##       t Compartments   Value
##   <dbl> <chr>          <dbl>
## 1     0 S            9999   
## 2     0 I               1   
## 3     0 R               0   
## 4     1 S            9999.  
## 5     1 I               1.20
## 6     1 R               0.1
# Plot
ggplot(data2c_long, aes(x = t, y = Value, colour = Compartments))+
  geom_line(size = 1)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("S" = "#0000EE",
                                 "I" = "#EE3B3B",
                                 "R" = "#00EE00"),
                      labels = c("S" = "Susceptible",
                                 "I" = "Infectious",
                                 "R" = "Recovered/Removed"))+
  labs(
    x = "Time (t)",
    y = "Cases",
    colour = "",
  )+
  theme_minimal()
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

3

data3_org <- data.frame(
  x = c(0, 5, 7, 13, 19, 25, 32),
  y = c(0, 3, 4, 9, 14, 11, 5)
)
head(data3_org)
##    x  y
## 1  0  0
## 2  5  3
## 3  7  4
## 4 13  9
## 5 19 14
## 6 25 11
data3_spline <- read.csv("./output_data/Q3_spline.csv", header = TRUE)
head(data3_spline)
##     x          y
## 1 0.0 0.00000000
## 2 0.1 0.06487958
## 3 0.2 0.12974744
## 4 0.3 0.19459188
## 5 0.4 0.25940116
## 6 0.5 0.32416358
data3_coefs <- read.csv("./output_data/Q3_coefs.csv", header = TRUE)
head(data3_coefs)
##   spline             a            b          c  d
## 1      0 -1.952613e-03  0.000000000  0.6488153  0
## 2      1  1.405226e-02 -0.029289192  0.5023694  3
## 3      2 -1.407012e-03  0.055024347  0.5538397  4
## 4      3 -1.130639e-02  0.029698139  1.0621746  9
## 5      4  9.595539e-03 -0.173816902  0.1974620 14
## 6      5  5.224755e-05 -0.001097199 -0.8520226 11
ggplot()+
  geom_line(data = data3_spline, aes(x = x, y = y), colour = "steelblue1")+
  geom_point(data = data3_org, aes(x = x, y = y), colour = "lightcoral", size = 3)+
  geom_text_repel(data = data3_org,
                  aes(x = x, y = y, label = paste0("(", x, ",", y, ")")),
                  size = 3.5)+
  theme_minimal()

data3_coefs |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., digits = 3)))
##   spline      a      b      c  d
## 1      0 -0.002  0.000  0.649  0
## 2      1  0.014 -0.029  0.502  3
## 3      2 -0.001  0.055  0.554  4
## 4      3 -0.011  0.030  1.062  9
## 5      4  0.010 -0.174  0.197 14
## 6      5  0.000 -0.001 -0.852 11

6b

# Read data
data6b <- read.csv("output_data/Q6b.csv", header = TRUE) # simulated (predicted)
head(data6b)
##   t_day   case_pred case_obs
## 1     7   0.4833827       70
## 2    14   1.9601532      223
## 3    21   7.9481761     1717
## 4    28  32.2224039     4895
## 5    35 130.5256263     8295
## 6    42 526.9956053    12175
# Plot
ggplot(data6b, aes(x = t_day)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = case_obs, colour = "Observed"), size = 2)+
  geom_line(aes(y = case_pred, colour = "Predicted, no GD"), size = 1)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Observed" = "lightcoral", 
                                "Predicted, no GD" = "steelblue1"))+
  labs(
    x = "Time (days)",
    y = "Cases",
    color = ""
  )+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

6e.

data6e <- read.csv("output_data/Q6e.csv", header = TRUE)
head(data6e)
##   t_day    case_pred case_obs
## 1     7     9.415082       70
## 2    14   173.660097      223
## 3    21  1177.437945     1717
## 4    28  4011.412404     4895
## 5    35  8437.916131     8295
## 6    42 12593.840287    12175
# Plot
ggplot(data6e, aes(x = t_day))+
  geom_point(aes(y = case_obs, colour = "Observed"), size = 2)+
  geom_line(aes(y = case_pred, colour = "Predicted, with GD"), size = 1)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Observed" = "lightcoral",
                                 "Predicted, with GD" = "steelblue1"))+
  labs(
    x = "Time (days)",
    y = "Cases",
    colour = ""
  )+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

 data6_combined <- data6b|>
  rename(case_initial = case_pred)|>
  left_join(data6e|>
              select(t_day, case_pred)|>
              rename(case_final = case_pred),
            by = "t_day")
head(data6_combined)
##   t_day case_initial case_obs   case_final
## 1     7    0.4833827       70     9.415082
## 2    14    1.9601532      223   173.660097
## 3    21    7.9481761     1717  1177.437945
## 4    28   32.2224039     4895  4011.412404
## 5    35  130.5256263     8295  8437.916131
## 6    42  526.9956053    12175 12593.840287
# Plot
ggplot(data6_combined, aes(x = t_day))+
  geom_point(aes(y = case_obs, colour = "Observed"), size = 3)+
  geom_line(aes(y = case_initial, colour = "Predicted, without gradient descent"), size = 1)+
  geom_line(aes(y = case_final, colour = "Predicted, with gradient descent"), size = 1)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Observed" = "#EE2C2C",
                                 "Predicted, without gradient descent" = "#79CDCD",
                                 "Predicted, with gradient descent" = "#EEC900"))+
  labs(
    x = "Time (days)",
    y = "Cases",
    colour = ""
  )+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(
    legend.position = "top"
  )

7.

# Data
data7 <- read.csv("output_data/Q7a.csv", header = TRUE)
head(data7)
##            x     y_true      y_pred
## 1  0.8399971 -151.88729    1.138603
## 2  1.2056846  -26.55968    9.367887
## 3 -0.4352525   81.87023   -2.580061
## 4  4.3020870  436.38255  359.074408
## 5  6.8971226 1418.54283 1305.833496
## 6  1.3741968   33.56986   14.722287
# Check range
range <- max(data7$y_true) - min(data7$y_true)
cat("Predicted case range:", range, "\n")
## Predicted case range: 5247.115
# Compute RMSE and relative error
mse <- 11136.385001392886
rmse <- sqrt(mse)
relative_error = rmse/range
cat("Relative error", relative_error)
## Relative error 0.02011183
# Plot
ggplot(data7, aes(x = x))+
  geom_line(aes(y = y_pred, colour = "Predicted"), linewidth = 1)+
  geom_point(aes(y = y_true, colour = "Observed"), linewidth = 1)+
  labs(
    x = "x",
    y = "y",
    colour = ""
  )+
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = max(data7$x)*0.6,
    y = 2500,
    label = paste("MSE = ", round(mse, 2), "\nRMSE = ", round(rmse, 2)),
    size = 4
  )+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")
## Warning in geom_point(aes(y = y_true, colour = "Observed"), linewidth = 1):
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `linewidth`

8

# Data
data8a <- read.csv("output_data/Q8.csv")
head(data8a)
##            x         y City
## 1 25.3465271 65.216793    0
## 2 26.6718076 49.447783   34
## 3  0.5564765 26.337485    7
## 4  9.4792274 23.340668   23
## 5  9.8050220 11.295679   44
## 6 37.2404655  6.155129   38
# Plot
ggplot(data8a, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_path(colour = "steelblue1") +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  annotate("point", x = data8a$x[1], y = data8a$y[1],
           colour = "red", size = 3)+
  geom_text(aes(label = City), vjust = -0.5, size = 3)+
  theme_minimal()

9a

# Data
data9a <- read.csv("output_data/Q9a.csv", header = TRUE)
table(data9a$Country)
## 
##      Algeria          DRC        Egypt     Ethiopia        Kenya      Nigeria 
##           52          112          143          134           69          223 
## South Africa        Sudan     Tanzania       Uganda 
##           82           63           67           55
data9a_sampled <- data9a|>
  count(Country)|>
  mutate(prop_simulated = n/sum(n))|>
  select(-n)
head(data9a_sampled)
##    Country prop_simulated
## 1  Algeria          0.052
## 2      DRC          0.112
## 3    Egypt          0.143
## 4 Ethiopia          0.134
## 5    Kenya          0.069
## 6  Nigeria          0.223
# Empirical data
data_emp <- data.frame(
  Country = c("Nigeria","Ethiopia","Egypt","DRC","Tanzania", "South Africa","Kenya","Sudan","Uganda","Algeria"),
  Count = c(228,129,115,106,67,63,55,50,49,46)
)
data_emp$prop_org <- data_emp$Count/sum(data_emp$Count) # compute probability
head(data_emp)
##        Country Count   prop_org
## 1      Nigeria   228 0.25110132
## 2     Ethiopia   129 0.14207048
## 3        Egypt   115 0.12665198
## 4          DRC   106 0.11674009
## 5     Tanzania    67 0.07378855
## 6 South Africa    63 0.06938326
# Merge datasets
data9a_comb <- data9a_sampled|>
  left_join(data_emp|>
              dplyr::select(Country, prop_org),
            by = "Country")|>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(prop_simulated, prop_org),
               names_to = "Type",
               values_to = "Value")
head(data9a_comb)
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   Country Type            Value
##   <chr>   <chr>           <dbl>
## 1 Algeria prop_simulated 0.052 
## 2 Algeria prop_org       0.0507
## 3 DRC     prop_simulated 0.112 
## 4 DRC     prop_org       0.117 
## 5 Egypt   prop_simulated 0.143 
## 6 Egypt   prop_org       0.127
# Plot
ggplot(data9a_comb, aes(x = reorder(Country, Value), y = Value, fill = Type))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("prop_simulated" = "#87CEFF",
                               "prop_org" = "grey70"),
                    labels = c("Sampled", "Original"))+
  labs(x = "Country",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")

9b

# Data
data9b <- read.csv("output_data/Q9b.csv", header = TRUE)
head(data9b)
##       x
## 1  9980
## 2 10083
## 3 10021
## 4 10132
## 5 10124
## 6  9981
# Plot

# binomial mean = (number or trials)*(probability of success)
mu <- 1e6*0.01
# binomial variance = np(1-p)
sigma <- sqrt(1e6 * 0.01 * (1 - 0.01)) 

ggplot(data9b, aes(x = x))+
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 30,
                 fill = "lightgrey",
                 colour = "black")+
  geom_density(aes(colour = "Estimated density"),
               linewidth = 1,
               key_glyph = "path")+
  stat_function(aes(colour = "Normal approximation"),
                fun = dnorm,
                args = list(mean = mu, sd = sigma),
                linewidth = 1)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Estimated density" = "blue",
                                 "Normal approximation" = "red"))+
  labs(x = "x",
       y = "Density",
       colour = "")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Data
data9b_gamma <- read.csv("output_data/Q9b_gamma.csv", header = TRUE)
head(data9b_gamma)
##       x
## 1 10063
## 2  9934
## 3  9978
## 4  9866
## 5 10198
## 6 10044
# Plot

# binomial mean = (number or trials)*(probability of success)
mu <- 1e6*0.01
# binomial variance = np(1-p)
sigma <- sqrt(1e6 * 0.01 * (1 - 0.01)) 

ggplot(data9b_gamma, aes(x = x))+
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 30,
                 fill = "lightgrey",
                 colour = "black")+
  geom_density(aes(colour = "Estimated density"),
               linewidth = 1,
               key_glyph = "path")+
  stat_function(aes(colour = "Normal approximation"),
                fun = dnorm,
                args = list(mean = mu, sd = sigma),
                linewidth = 1)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Estimated density" = "blue",
                                 "Normal approximation" = "red"))+
  labs(x = "x",
       y = "Density",
       colour = "")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")

9c

# Data
data9c <- read.csv("output_data/Q9c.csv", header = TRUE)
head(data9c)
##           x
## 1 0.7489134
## 2 0.7688497
## 3 0.6957342
## 4 0.8027612
## 5 0.7344741
## 6 0.8983601
# Plot
ggplot(data9c, aes(x = x))+
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 30,
                 fill = "lightgrey",
                 colour = "black")+
  geom_density(aes(colour = "Estimated density"),
               linewidth = 1,
               key_glyph = "path")+
  stat_function(aes(colour = "Theoretical Beta density"),
                fun = dbeta,
                args = list(shape1 = 9, shape2 = 3),
                linewidth = 1)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Estimated density" = "blue",
                                 "Theoretical Beta density" = "red"))+
  labs(x = "x",
       y = "Density",
       colour = "")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")

9d

# Data
data9d <- read.csv("output_data/Q9d.csv", header = TRUE)
head(data9d)
##           x
## 1 -1.766918
## 2 -4.509489
## 3 -3.370672
## 4 -2.446180
## 5 -7.234261
## 6 -9.385975
# Plot
z <- integrate(function(x) 5*cos(x^2) - 2*x + 25, lower = -10, upper = 10)$value

ggplot(data9d, aes(x = x))+
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 30,
                 fill = "lightgrey",
                 colour = "black")+
  geom_density(aes(colour = "Estimated density"),
               linewidth = 1, fill = NA,
               key_glyph = "path")+
  stat_function(aes(colour = "Normalised target density"),
                fun = function(x) (5*cos(x^2) - 2*x + 25)/z,
                linewidth = 1)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Estimated density" = "blue",
                                 "Normalised target density" = "red"))+
  labs(x = "x",
       y = "Density",
       colour = "")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")

9e

# Data
data9e <- read.csv("output_data/Q9e.csv")
head(data9e)
##          A         B
## 1 30.15484  96.54952
## 2 27.31807  92.46482
## 3 31.62425  98.86520
## 4 26.41441  91.22371
## 5 31.67336 100.70730
## 6 23.03941  85.93107
# Plot A
ggplot(data9e, aes(x = A))+
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 30,
                 fill = "lightgrey",
                 colour = "black")+
  geom_density(aes(colour = "Estimated density"),
               key_glyph = "path")+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Estimated density" = "blue"))+
  labs(x = "A", y = "Density", colour = "")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Plot B
ggplot(data9e, aes(x = B))+
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 30,
                 fill = "lightgrey",
                 colour = "black")+
  geom_density(aes(colour = "Estimated density"),
               key_glyph = "path")+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Estimated density" = "blue"))+
  labs(x = "B", y = "Density", colour = "")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Scatterplot
rho_val <- round(cor(data9e$A, data9e$B), 3)

ggplot(data9e, aes(x = A, y = B))+
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, colour = "steelblue1")+
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              se = FALSE,
              aes(colour = "Regression line"),
              linewidth = 1)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Regression line" = "red"))+
  labs(x = "A", y = "B", colour = "")+
  annotate("text",
           x = quantile(data9e$A, 0.05),,
           y = quantile(data9e$B, 0.95),
           label = as.expression(bquote(rho == .(rho_val))),
           hjust = 0,
           vjust = 1)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
